2018-11-24.abstract: The “Yellow Jackets” movement protesting rising fuel prices is among the most serious challenges yet to President Emmanuel Macron’s pro-business government.

2018-11-25.title: PETA Asks a Village Named Wool to Change Its Name to Vegan Wool
2018-11-25.abstract: When the animal rights group sent a letter asking the English village to take a stand against animal cruelty, residents were surprised, and not amused.

2018-11-25.title: Leda and the Swan: A Fresco Comes to Life in Pompeii Excavation
2018-11-25.abstract: An expensive excavation campaign to shore up an at-risk area of the city is a constant font of archaeological discoveries.

2018-11-25.title: E.U. Leaders and U.K. Agree on Brexit Divorce Terms
2018-11-25.abstract: The settlement reached at a summit meeting on Sunday must pass votes in the British Parliament, and leaves deep uncertainties about Britain’s future.

2018-11-25.title: China’s Economy Became No. 2 by Defying No. 1
2018-11-25.abstract: The Chinese thought they had little to learn from conventional wisdom. Now it’s the West that’s taking notes.

2018-11-25.title: How China’s Rulers Control Society: Opportunity, Nationalism, Fear
2018-11-25.abstract: As China grew richer, the West assumed, political freedoms would follow. Now it is an economic superpower — and the opposite has happened.

2018-11-26.title: Ukraine, After Naval Clash With Russia, Considers Martial Law
2018-11-26.abstract: Ukraine said Russia opened fire Sunday on its ships in the Black Sea. The United Nations Security Council is convening an emergency meeting about it.

2018-11-26.title: As Migrants Try to Cross Border, U.S. Closes It
2018-11-26.abstract: Hundreds of migrants tried to evade the police and cross from Tijuana, Mexico, into San Diego. The U.S. Customs and Border Protection Agency responded by firing tear gas and closing the border in both directions. Thousands of migrants from Central America have been living in a shelter in Tijuana.

2018-11-26.title: More Than 140 Whales Die in New Zealand After Mass Beaching
2018-11-26.abstract: Such mass beachings can happen when whales make navigational mistakes while chasing prey, escaping predators or trying to protect sick members of the group.

2018-11-26.title: Matthew Hedges, British Student Accused of Spying, Is Pardoned by U.A.E.
2018-11-26.abstract: The conviction of Mr. Hedges, and his sentence of life in prison, had been met with outrage in Britain.

2018-11-26.title: Turkey Searches Saudi-Owned Mansion for Evidence in Khashoggi Case
2018-11-26.abstract: Investigators still have not found the remains of Jamal Khashoggi, the dissident who was killed inside the Saudi Consulate in Istanbul.

2018-11-27.title: Pain and Sense of Betrayal as Canada G.M. Factory Closes After a Century
2018-11-27.abstract: “Just as you’re getting older to know that you don’t have a job, it’s really heart wrenching,” said a worker at the vast complex in Oshawa, Ontario, where families worked for G.M. for generations.

2018-11-27.title: Facing Attack, George Soros’s Foundation Will Shut Down in Turkey
2018-11-27.abstract: Open Society Foundations plans to close its office in Istanbul. It claims that the Turkish government has falsely accused it of playing a role in uprisings.

2018-11-27.title: Honduran President’s Brother, Arrested in Miami, Is Charged With Drug Trafficking
2018-11-27.abstract: Juan Antonio Hernández, the brother of the Honduran president, was described as a “large-scale drug trafficker” with ties to organized crime and cocaine deliveries to the United States.

2018-11-27.title: Music Festivals Are Gaining Popularity in Asia. Just Not With Officials.
2018-11-27.abstract: Conservative governments have been canceling Western-inspired events, which they see as threats to public safety, political stability or social and religious values.

2018-11-27.title: A Journalist Was Killed in Mexico. Then His Colleagues Were Hacked.
2018-11-27.abstract: Text messages sent to them were infected with a spyware that the Mexican government bought from an Israeli cyber arms dealer, according to a forensic analysis.

2018-11-28.title: Raising Pressure on Nicaragua, U.S. Imposes Sanctions on Vice President
2018-11-28.abstract: Vice president and first lady Rosario Murillo, along with a top aide, was targeted with penalties aimed at ending a harsh crackdown.

2018-11-28.title: Explosion Near Chemical Plant in China Kills at Least 23
2018-11-28.abstract: Fire engulfed dozens of trucks near the plant in Hebei Province after a blast that could be heard more than a mile away. The cause was unclear.

2018-11-28.title: Lawyer for Snowden in Hong Kong Says He Left City Under Pressure
2018-11-28.abstract: Robert Tibbo, a Canadian lawyer, says he is being punished for bringing attention to the plight of asylum seekers in Hong Kong, some of whom sheltered Edward J. Snowden.

2018-11-28.title: In Australia, a Sudanese Basketball League Finds Itself Sidelined by Racist Fears
2018-11-28.abstract: The South Sudanese Australian National Basketball Association said it was forced to cancel a tournament after being unfairly linked to stories of gang violence.

2018-11-28.title: New Zealand Blocks Huawei, in Blow to Chinese Telecom Giant
2018-11-28.abstract: The announcement came a day after Papua New Guinea said it would uphold a deal with Huawei, illustrating the growing global divide over the risks of Chinese technology.

2018-11-29.title: Border Agents Shot Tear Gas Into Mexico. Was It Legal?
2018-11-29.abstract: Rights experts say the tear gas canisters fired into Mexico by American border protection forces violated Mexican sovereignty under international law. The weapons may also have blocked asylum seekers from exercising their legal rights.

2018-11-29.title: South Korean Court Orders Mitsubishi of Japan to Pay for Forced Wartime Labor
2018-11-29.abstract: The ruling against Mitsubishi Heavy Industries is likely to further complicate ties between America’s two key allies in Asia.

2018-11-29.title: 3 Philippine Police Officers Are Convicted in a Drug War Killing
2018-11-29.abstract: Three officers were sentenced to 40 years in prison in the killing of a 17-year-old boy. Eyes are now on President Rodrigo Duterte, who had sworn to pardon guilty officers.

2018-11-29.title: Wildfires Burn Across Eastern Australia in Sweltering Heat Wave
2018-11-29.abstract: The wildfires marked the first time the Queensland government rated the danger “catastrophic” in the state’s history. Thousands of people have been evacuated.

2018-11-29.title: North Korea Accuses Washington of Weaponizing Human Rights as Nuclear Talks Stall
2018-11-29.abstract: President Trump may have said he and Kim Jong-un were “in love,” but the two countries are sparring over the North’s human rights record.

2018-11-30.title: For First Time in a Decade, a South Korean Train Rolls Into the North
2018-11-30.abstract: Officials and engineers from both Koreas will spend 18 days on the six-car train, riding the North’s decrepit rail network and considering how it could be improved.

2018-11-30.title: As Measles Surges, ‘Decades of Progress’ Are in Jeopardy
2018-11-30.abstract: Health officials expressed alarm about a rebound in measles, once nearly eradicated in many regions. Reported cases surged by nearly a third worldwide.

2018-11-30.title: Seven Convicted in Killing of Prominent Honduran Environmentalist
2018-11-30.abstract: The opposition to a dam project by an indigenous leader, Berta Cáceres, had brought her international prominence but failed to protect her life.

2018-11-30.title: South Korea Frees 58 Conscientious Objectors in Wake of Landmark Ruling
2018-11-30.abstract: South Korea has imprisoned thousands of men for refusing mandatory military service. But the practice is coming to an end after a Supreme Court ruling this month.

2018-11-30.title: Cuban Doctors Accuse International Agency of Profiting From Their Work
2018-11-30.abstract: A division of the World Health Organization made about $75 million off the work of Cuban doctors who earned substandard wages in Brazil, a lawsuit alleges.
